compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: E1 DESCOPE NOTE (2026-07-16): E1's finding-adjudication TP/FP corpus was INVALIDATED (tool-less, plan-truncated labeling that doesn't match the whole-plan/code-grounded/agentic production gate; ~79 percent contaminated) and DESCOPED — the independent human-adjudicated gold set is parked as idea e59d-c078-724d-46e5. E1 still delivers the VALID outcome corpus + finding sample. This story (R2) runs its lexicon over the historical AC corpus from E1's OUTCOME corpus tickets. UNAFFECTED; proceed on the outcome corpus when resourced.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Two work tickets (115b, 8c4f) burned close-gate cycles: their acceptance
criteria had "done" evidence that lives OUTSIDE the codebase (live-store fsck
surgery, phantom-dir deletion, changes landed through Gerrit) but were NOT tagged
`[operator-attested]` (ADR-0043), so the completion verifier failed looking for
code proof until the tag was added retroactively. A cheap DETERMINISTIC pass-1
lint that surfaces this at PLAN time — before claim — removes the whole
round-trip. Rule-based lexicon checks are the proven high-recall/cheap tier for
requirement-defect shape (AQUSA-grade prior art); this project already ships two
such lexicon lints in the same P6/P7 family (`p6_ac_quality`'s vague-term +
verify-command lint at `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py:686`, and
`p7_destructive` at `:762`), so this is a well-precedented extension, not a new
mechanism.

## What

Add an ADVISORY, prompt-less DETERMINISTIC lint (ADR-0016 `exec: DET`, the P6/AC
family) that flags AC checklist items whose text carries operational-evidence
markers (deploy / prod / live-run / IaC-apply / cloud-state / merge-gate /
human-action / drill / store-surgery / recorded-attestation) but which are NOT
tagged `[operator-attested]`. It NEVER blocks (advisory coaching only).

### Design decisions (grounded against the code)

- **It EXTENDS `p6_ac_quality`; it does NOT add a `P10` and does NOT touch
  `criteria_routing.json`.** The static DET floor (`DET_CHECKS`,
  `det_floor.py:880`) is hardcoded P1–P9 and enumerated in
  `registry.py:78 CANONICAL_DET`; DET checks are NOT routed through
  `criteria_routing.json`. `registry.validate_packaged_routing()`
  (`registry.py:445-446`) flags any routing key not in `CANONICAL_LLM` as an
  ORPHAN and fails CI, so a packaged DET routing entry is impossible without a
  larger, out-of-scope change. And `.rebar/`-overlay `exec:DET` criteria run
  through `det_invariants.run_project_det_checks` (`det_invariants.py:207`),
  which scans CODE with a grounding detector (`engine_b`) — structurally wrong
  for an AC-TEXT lexicon. The existing regression test
  `tests/unit/test_det_invariants.py:327` already pins "the lint EXTENDS p6 …
  DET_CHECKS stays exactly P1-P9", exactly matching how the verify-command lint
  (G-3a) was folded into p6. So the correct home is `p6_ac_quality`, keeping
  `DET_CHECKS` at 9 and `CANONICAL_DET` unchanged. The task's suggestion of a
  `criteria_routing.json` entry is refuted by the code and is deliberately NOT
  done; `p6_ac_quality` already ships in the packaged registry (no config flag),
  satisfying "advisory in packaged registry".

- **Single-source the tag matcher.** The canonical `[operator-attested]` matcher
  is `workflow_ops._OPERATOR_ATTESTED_AC_RE` (`workflow_ops.py:61`), consumed by
  the completion-verifier enrichment path (`workflow_ops.py:80
  enrich_operator_attested`, `passes.py:769`). `det_floor` imports nothing from
  `workflow_ops`, and `workflow_ops` imports `det_floor` lazily
  (`workflow_ops.py:135`), so `det_floor` will OWN the regex as
  `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE` and `workflow_ops` will import it — no import cycle,
  one source of truth, so the lint and the verifier agree on "tagged" by
  construction (guarded by a unit test).

- **Precision-first (advisory posture).** An advisory lint must not nag, so the
  lexicon is tuned for precision with a codebase-verifiable SUPPRESSION co-signal
  (drop items that name an in-repo proving command / test / doc / config-file
  deliverable, or carry a "… — landed on main" trailer) and a NEGATION guard
  ("no live", "read-only provisioning"). Recall is a reported figure, not a gate.

### Self-gate (DETERMINISTIC — no LLM anywhere)

Run the locked lexicon over the frozen historical AC corpus committed under
`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/`. The corpus is the AC checklist items
of the 556 E1 outcome-corpus tickets that resolve in the store
(`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl` → 3091 AC items,
31 already tagged `[operator-attested]`). Ground-truth for precision is a frozen
per-item TP/FP adjudication of the full flagged set (a census ≥ the required
50-item sample); recall is measured against the 31 tagged items with their tag
stripped. Measured on this corpus: flag rate 2.07% (≤5%), recall 61% (19/31),
precision ~92% (census of 64 flagged items, 6 adjudicated false-positive), and
both known cases (115b, 8c4f) fire. NO LLM call runs in the eval or the lint.

### Landing order (pre-registration, git-ancestry)

Landed as a 2-commit Gerrit stack so the eval is committed BEFORE the lint is
active: commit 1 adds the frozen corpus + the harness + the committed `eval.json`
result and the detector helper `_operator_evidence_ac_gaps()` in `det_floor.py`
in DORMANT form (defined, used only by the harness, not yet called by
`p6_ac_quality`); commit 2 wires the helper into `p6_ac_quality` (activation),
adds the unit tests + the `workflow_ops` single-source import, and documents the
lint in `docs/plan-review-gate.md`.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py` — the `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE`,
  the operational-evidence lexicon (marker families + suppression + negation),
  the pure helper `_operator_evidence_ac_gaps(text) -> list[(ac_line, markers)]`,
  and its wiring into `p6_ac_quality` (append one advisory coaching issue per
  gap; never changes P6's non-blocking status).
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py` — import
  `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE` from `det_floor` (drop the local duplicate).
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/operator_attested_eval.py` — the
  deterministic eval harness (imports the shipped helper; runs over the frozen
  corpus; computes flag rate / recall / known-case firing; loads frozen precision
  adjudications; asserts the gate).
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/operator_attested_ac_corpus.jsonl` —
  the frozen AC corpus.
- `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/operator_attested_eval.json` — the
  committed eval result (precision/recall/flag-rate/known-cases + adjudications +
  PASS verdict).
- `tests/unit/test_det_floor_operator_attested.py` — table-driven unit tests over
  labeled AC strings (115b/8c4f fixtures fire; negative controls do not;
  already-tagged items do not; agreement with `workflow_ops`).
- `docs/plan-review-gate.md` — document the lint (advisory P6-family DET,
  ADR-0016/0043).

## Out of scope

- Any `criteria_routing.json` change (refuted by the code, see above).
- A new `P10` DET check or any change to `CANONICAL_DET` / `DET_CHECKS`
  cardinality.
- Blocking posture, LLM rubric, or an `.rebar/` overlay entry.
- Any human-adjudicated gold set (parked as idea e59d).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The deterministic lexicon eval is committed under
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/operator_attested_eval.json` BEFORE the
  lint is activated (its commit is a git ancestor of the p6 activation commit),
  recording sampled precision ≥ 70% over a ≥ 50-item flagged sample AND overall
  flag rate ≤ 5% over the frozen corpus, plus the recall figure. Proof:
  `python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/operator_attested_eval.json')); assert d['precision']['precision_pct']>=70 and d['precision']['sample_size']>=50 and d['flag_rate_pct']<=5.0 and d['gate']['verdict']=='PASS', d"`
  and `git merge-base --is-ancestor <eval-commit> <activation-commit>` exits 0.
  (If the eval had failed the thresholds, the story closes deferred with the
  failing result recorded instead.)
- [ ] Both known cases fire on their historical (tag-stripped) AC text, pinned as
  unit-test fixtures, and an already-tagged item does NOT fire. Proof:
  `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_det_floor_operator_attested.py -q` passes,
  including `test_known_case_115b_fires`, `test_known_case_8c4f_fires`, and
  `test_tagged_item_not_flagged`.
- [ ] The lint ships ADVISORY and prompt-less in the packaged floor: it extends
  `p6_ac_quality` (which never blocks), `DET_CHECKS` stays exactly 9 and
  `CANONICAL_DET` is unchanged, and no `criteria_routing.json` entry is added.
  Proof: `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review.det_floor import DET_CHECKS; assert len(DET_CHECKS)==9"`;
  `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review import registry,det_floor; assert list(registry.CANONICAL_DET)==['P1','P2','P3','P4','P5','P6','P7','P8','P9']; assert registry.validate_packaged_routing()==[]"`;
  and a unit test asserting a `p6_ac_quality` result on an untagged operational
  AC has `status=='fail'` with `blocking is False`.
- [ ] The lint and the completion-verifier agree on "tagged": `det_floor` owns
  `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE` and `workflow_ops` imports it. Proof:
  `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review.det_floor import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE as a; from rebar.llm.plan_review.workflow_ops import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_AC_RE as b; assert a is b"`
  and a unit test asserting `_operator_evidence_ac_gaps` never flags a line that
  `workflow_ops.operator_attested_ac_texts` recognizes as tagged.
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` documents the new advisory P6-family DET lint
  (what it flags, that it is advisory/prompt-less, its self-gate result). Proof:
  `grep -qi "operator-attested" docs/plan-review-gate.md`.
- [ ] The whole suite stays green: `make lint && make typecheck && make test`
  pass (or the targeted `det_floor` / `plan_review` / `criteria_overlay` subsets
  plus the module-size gate).
- [ ] [operator-attested] The change is landed on `main` through Gerrit
  (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1, no unresolved comments), attested on this
  ticket with the Gerrit change number(s) and the merge commit sha.

file_impact.reason: operator-attested advisory DET lint (extends p6_ac_quality) + shared tag regex + detector helper
import the shared _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE from det_floor (single source)
table-driven unit tests: known cases fire, negatives/tagged do not, verifier agreement
committed eval result (>=70% precision, <=5% flag rate, recall)
document the advisory P6-family DET lint

compiled_state.title: R2: operator-attested evidence-kind DET lint (P6 family, advisory)
